commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: test: guard against invalid flag values in production flags.json
commits.body:
JsonFile::get_flags() silently drops flags whose value is not 'on'/'off',
which then makes flagIsOn() throw at runtime and breaks the UI that reads
the flag (recent ENABLE_AI_TOOL_CALLS_DISPLAY = "yes" incident). Catch
those at PR time with a guard assertion over every entry in flags.json.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: chore: whoops, set the flag to `yes` instead of `on`

commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: fix(ETC-771): Ensure image preview updates properly in MediaPicker

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: fix(AI Chat): recover streaming finalized content from done events
commits.body:
Handle middleware/OpenAI stream cases where the only usable content arrives in final `done` events instead of earlier
delta events.

- forward finalized text when middleware skips text deltas
- salvage finalized reasoning before the completion marker
- avoid duplicate output when the same segment already streamed via delta
- add regression coverage for fallback and duplicate-suppression cases

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: refactor(AI Chat): unify auxiliary items for chronological rendering
commits.body:
Replace assistant message `reasoning` and `toolCalls` buckets with a single ordered `auxiliaryItems` union so AI chat
metadata renders in chronological stream order instead of grouped by type.

- store assistant reasoning/tool-call metadata in ordered `auxiliaryItems`
- keep tool calls batched per `client_tool_calls` SSE event
- preserve reasoning sentinel behavior for streamed reasoning boundaries
- normalize legacy localStorage chat messages during deserialization
- remove grouped rendering logic in `AiChatContent` and render one ordered loop
- update store and UI tests to cover ordering, continuation streams, and migration
- extract `#lastItemIsOpenReasoningSlot()` to simplify `finishReasoning()` and resolve the CodeScene complex conditional
  warning

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: ETC-762: improve contract & coverage for 'find_media_library_items'
commits.body:
- Satisfy Codescene
- cover edge cases
- differentiate between "nothing found" and "offset too big"
- make tool implementation more defensive

commits.author: Jérémy Franck
commits.subject: feat(AI chat): improve media library lookup tool contract + rename to `find_media_library_items`
commits.body:
- rename `list_media_library_items` -> `find_media_library_items`
- fetch only the media fields the tool needs to reduce response size
- return pagination metadata needed for large libraries
- make follow-up fetches reliable with `offset` + `next_offset`
- add tests for tool contract, registration, and media query params

ETC-762.title: AI is able to find media from the media library
ETC-762.description:
Create a basic tool that lets AI find media from the media library and return the info needed to be used in Etch.

The tool should return:

* `id`
* `title`
* `source_url`
* `mime_type`

## Acceptance criteria

- [X] When the media library is empty, or a valid search returns no matches, the tool returns an empty `items` array with `offset=0`, `returned_count=0`, `next_offset=0`, `total=0`, `total_pages=0`
- [X] I can ask AI to find items from library, and it will be able to see them and get required fields for actual usage (include `id`, `url`, `title`, `mime_type`, and `media_type`, plus pagination metadata: `offset`, `limit`, `returned_count`, `next_offset`, `total`, `total_pages`). This works for one item, as well as multiple items.
- [X] If I ask AI to query less than 1 item, or more than 100, it receives an error code (`INVALID_ARGS`) and a useful error message
- [X] AI cannot query with a negative `offset`. If it did, it would receive an error code (`INVALID_ARGS`) and a useful error message
- [X] When AI queries with an offset beyond the available media items (e.g. `offset=61` on a Media Library that only contains 60 items), the tool returns an empty `items` arrray and sets `total=null` and `total_pages=null` to signal an out-of-range pagination request
- [X] When AI receives an item with a null or missing title, it renders as `""` rather than failing
- [X] AI cannot query with an empty (or whitespace-characetrs only) search string. If it did, it would receive an `INVALID_ARGS` error.
- [X] AI can only query media type of `image` (for now). It it tried another media type, it would receive a `INVALID_ARGS` error.
- [X] AI can combine `search` + `media_type` + `offset` and receive a correctly shaped paginated response

## Technical details

* `limit` has a minimum of 1 (otherwise it won't return anything) and a maximum of 100 (limitation from the WP REST API)
* `offset=-1` has a minimum of 0 (it doesn't make sense to offset a negative number)
* We will use `_fields=id,title,source_url,mime_type,media_type` in the WP REST API query to reduce response size
* Pagination totals are read WP REST API Response headers

ETC-665.title: Component in slot does not display content preview
ETC-665.description:
If you have a component nested in the slot of another component, the content preview seems to break.

## This is with the preview feature toggled on - it just shows the prop data keys, not the actual values (default or otherwise).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add an instance of a component with a slot
2. Add another component instance in the slot from step 1
3. Edit the component instance from step 2
4. Notice the canvas content preview does not show content, it shows the raw keys e.g. {props.someText}

---

### Environment

Plugin Version: 1.4.11

Theme Version: 0.0.7

WordPress Version: 6.9.3

Machine: Mac

Browser: Chromium

---

### Magic Login

---

### Attachments

[https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/bug-component-in-slot-does-not-display-content-preview](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/general-discussion/bug-component-in-slot-does-not-display-content-preview>)

---

### Reporter

Name: Dan Woolloff (Strange Tech)

Email: dev@strange.tech

ETC-756.title: Add `supported_client_capabilities` negotiation for AI tool schemas
ETC-756.description:
**Summary**
Introduce a `supported_client_capabilities` array from plugin → middleware so the middleware can advertise tool definitions that match the client’s released capabilities (e.g., expanded `create_posts` schema), keeping trunk-based middleware deploys safe for older plugin versions.

Keys in `supported_client_capabilities` are to be used as feature flags to construct the tools schemas for LLMs

---


**Given that** AI tools negotiation is enabled and the client can declare capabilities

- [X] **When** the plugin sends a chat request without `supported_client_capabilities`, **then** middleware behavior remains backwards compatible with today’s tool definitions.
- [X] **When** the plugin sends `supported_client_capabilities` as a list of strings, **then** the middleware accepts the request and uses the capabilities to shape tool definitions it advertises to the LLM.
- [X] **When** the plugin sends `supported_client_capabilities` as an empty array, **then** the middleware treats it as “no capabilities” and uses the baseline tool definitions.
- [X] **When** the plugin sends `supported_client_capabilities` with invalid entries (non-strings), **then** the middleware returns a coded negotiation error.
- [X] **When** the plugin declares a capability that affects a tool schema (e.g., `create_posts` fields), **then** the LLM sees the tool definition that matches the declared capability.
- [X] **When** the plugin does not declare a capability for a tool schema change, **then** the LLM sees the older/safer tool definition for that tool.
- [X] **When** the plugin includes `supported_client_capabilities`, **then** it is forwarded through REST → provider → middleware payloads in both initial and continue requests.

---

## **Technical context**

* Frontend `AiChat.svelte.ts` already adds `supported_client_tools` to initial/continue payloads when `ENABLE_AI_TOOLS_NEGOTIATION` is on. The new field should mirror this pattern.
* REST route `AiRoutes.php` and provider `MiddlewareProvider.php` already sanitize and forward `supported_client_tools`; same plumbing is needed for `supported_client_capabilities`.
* Middleware `schema.ts`, `service.ts`, `toolsNegotiation.ts`, and `toolsRegistry.ts` already implement tool negotiation. Capabilities should influence tool schema selection there.
* Existing tests for tools negotiation exist across frontend, plugin, and middleware; the new field should follow the same testing patterns.
